label: Program synthesis & code search

node.label: Program synthesis & code search
node.definition: Program synthesis searches a space of executable programs from specifications, examples, tests, types, natural-language intent, or learned priors.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Program synthesis & code search is to connect Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* to downstream capabilities including Code models, copilots & repository intelligence.
examples: substrate for Code models, copilots & repository intelligence
combined with Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*.

dependencies.rationale: Program synthesis & code search uses Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Program synthesis & code search uses Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Program synthesis & code search uses Decoder-only causal transformers as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Program synthesis & code search uses Constraint propagation, logic & program execution as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
